Commit 3bfb1a9: feat: Complete planning workflow with DRY command system and strategic analytics

payload.commit_message:
feat: Complete planning workflow with DRY command system and strategic analytics

## Major Features

1. **Dependency-Based Analytics**
   - Strategic planning methods in SDK and AgentInterface
   - find_bottlenecks(), recommend_next_work(), get_parallel_work()
   - assess_risks(), analyze_impact()
   - All methods return agent-friendly dicts

2. **Planning Workflow Integration**
   - smart_plan() - Context-aware spike or track creation
   - start_planning_spike() - Timeboxed research spikes
   - create_track_from_plan() - Convert spike findings to tracks
   - Complete workflow: Recommend → Plan → Spike → Track → Features

3. **DRY Command System**
   - 14 YAML command definitions (single source of truth)
   - Generator script creates platform-specific files
   - 42 command files generated for 3 platforms
   - ~2000+ lines of duplication eliminated

4. **Enhanced Start Command**
   - Integrated dependency analytics at session start
   - Shows bottlenecks, recommendations, parallel capacity
   - Data-driven work suggestions

## Files Changed

- SDK methods: sdk.py (smart_plan, start_planning_spike, create_track_from_plan)
- Agent methods: agents.py (find_bottlenecks, recommend_next_work, etc.)
- Analytics: dependency_analytics.py, dependency_models.py
- Commands: 14 YAML definitions → 42 generated files
- Docs: PLANNING_WORKFLOW.md, AGENT_STRATEGIC_PLANNING.md
- Skills: Updated for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini

## Platform Support

✅ Claude Code - All commands available
✅ Codex - All commands available
✅ Gemini - All commands available

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
https://claude.com/claude-code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Commit 0873979: feat(crud): Complete SDK CRUD operations with delete methods

payload.commit_message:
feat(crud): Complete SDK CRUD operations with delete methods

Implements full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) support across
HtmlGraph and SDK Collection interfaces.

Changes:
- Add HtmlGraph.delete() method (CRUD alias for remove())
- Add HtmlGraph.batch_delete() for vectorized deletions
- Add Collection.batch_delete() for SDK interface
- Automatic edge cleanup on deletion (incoming + outgoing)
- 16 comprehensive unit tests (all passing)
- Updated SDK documentation with delete examples

All collection types now support delete:
- features, bugs, chores, spikes, epics, phases

Commit a1273e9: feat(examples): Complete Phase 3 with 4 comprehensive examples

payload.commit_message:
feat(examples): Complete Phase 3 with 4 comprehensive examples

Implements all planned examples demonstrating HtmlGraph's core use cases.

Examples Created:
1. Todo List - Basic task management with graph operations
   - CSS selector queries
   - Graph traversal (dependencies, bottlenecks)
   - Agent interface demo
   - Programmatic node creation

2. Agent Coordination - Multi-agent collaboration (Ijoka use case)
   - Feature claiming and dependencies
   - 3 specialized agents (backend, frontend, fullstack)
   - Strategic recommendations
   - Parallel work discovery

3. Knowledge Base - Personal knowledge management
   - Interconnected concept notes
   - Bidirectional links
   - Knowledge graph queries
   - Like Obsidian/Roam, but pure HTML

4. Documentation Site - Static docs with no build step
   - References actual HtmlGraph docs
   - Shows real-world usage

Each example includes:
- Fully functional demo.py script
- Comprehensive README.md
- Working demonstrations

Feature: feat-5d75d781

Commit 37ea866: docs: Use imperative language and proper cross-references

payload.commit_message:
docs: Use imperative language and proper cross-references

CRITICAL CHANGES - Platform Documentation Synchronization:

1. ALL platform skill files NOW reference AGENTS.md
   - packages/claude-plugin/skills/htmlgraph-tracker/SKILL.md
   - packages/gemini-extension/GEMINI.md
   - packages/codex-skill/SKILL.md
   - Root-level GEMINI.md

2. Language changed to IMPERATIVE (DO THIS, NOT 'you can')
   - "REQUIRED READING" sections added
   - "ABSOLUTE RULE" for forbidden operations
   - "DO THIS FIRST" directives
   - ❌ FORBIDDEN vs ✅ REQUIRED patterns

3. Updated sync script to check ACTUAL file locations
   - Root-level: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md
   - Packages: skill files in proper platform directories
   - Validates all references to AGENTS.md

4. Cross-reference structure:
   AGENTS.md (central docs)
     ↑
     ├─ packages/claude-plugin/skills/htmlgraph-tracker/SKILL.md
     ├─ packages/gemini-extension/GEMINI.md
     ├─ packages/codex-skill/SKILL.md
     └─ Root GEMINI.md

Benefits:
✅ Single source of truth (AGENTS.md for SDK/deployment)
✅ Imperative commands (agents know what to DO)
✅ Proper file locations (matches actual platform structure)
✅ Automated sync validation (script checks all references)

Verified: python scripts/sync_memory_files.py --check
Output: ✅ All files are properly synchronized!

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Commit 18bc8c5: docs: Add critical dogfooding context and package roadmap

payload.commit_message:
docs: Add critical dogfooding context and package roadmap

DOGFOODING CLARITY - CRITICAL UPDATE:

Added explicit context that THIS PROJECT uses HtmlGraph to develop HtmlGraph.

Changes:
1. CLAUDE.md - New "DOGFOODING CONTEXT" section
   - Explains we're using HtmlGraph for HtmlGraph development
   - Distinguishes GENERAL vs PROJECT-SPECIFIC workflows
   - Lists workflows that should be packaged for users
   - Shows how .htmlgraph/ is both example AND real usage

2. AGENTS.md - Dogfooding note for AI agents
   - Brief context at top of file
   - Links to full CLAUDE.md explanation
   - Helps agents understand dual purpose

3. Created 4 NEW FEATURES (dogfooding in action):
   - feat-130780b2: Package deployment script pattern
   - feat-3522bdea: Package memory file sync tool
   - feat-62f83856: Improve htmlgraph init for setup
   - feat-f0138246: Package git hooks for tracking

WORKFLOWS TO PACKAGE FOR USERS:
⚠️ Deployment automation (deploy-all.sh pattern)
⚠️ Memory file sync (sync_memory_files.py)
⚠️ Project initialization (enhanced htmlgraph init)
⚠️ Git hooks (automatic tracking)

ALREADY PACKAGED:
✅ Feature/track/analytics workflows (SDK)
✅ Session management (hooks)
✅ Strategic planning (SDK analytics)

Why This Matters:
- Users understand .htmlgraph/ is REAL, not just demo
- Clear what's general-purpose vs HtmlGraph-specific
- Roadmap for packaging useful workflows
- Demonstrates dogfooding best practices

Current Status: 69 total nodes
- 29 features (4 new for packaging workflows)
- 23 done, 24 todo
- Tracking our own development!

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Commit 680fe71: feat: Add flexible deployment options to deploy-all.sh

payload.commit_message:
feat: Add flexible deployment options to deploy-all.sh

CRITICAL IMPROVEMENT - Fixes deployment script inflexibility:

Added 5 new flags to deploy-all.sh for flexible deployment:

FLAGS IMPLEMENTED:
✅ --docs-only     Only commit and push (skip build/publish)
✅ --build-only    Only build package (skip git/publish)
✅ --skip-pypi     Skip PyPI publishing
✅ --skip-plugins  Skip plugin updates
✅ --dry-run       Preview without executing
✅ --help          Show usage

USAGE EXAMPLES:
# Documentation changes (THIS COMMIT!)
./scripts/deploy-all.sh --docs-only

# Test release build
./scripts/deploy-all.sh 0.7.1 --skip-pypi --dry-run

# Build without publishing
./scripts/deploy-all.sh --build-only

# Full release (original behavior)
./scripts/deploy-all.sh 0.7.1

WHY THIS MATTERS:
❌ BEFORE: Had to use manual git commands for doc changes
✅ AFTER: One script for all deployment scenarios
✅ Dogfooding: Discovered this gap by using HtmlGraph

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Flag parsing with proper argument handling
- Conditional step execution
- Dry-run wrapper for preview mode
- Backward compatible (no flags = full release)
- Updated scripts/README.md with examples

TESTED:
✅ --help (shows usage)
✅ --dry-run (previews actions)
✅ --docs-only (git push only)
✅ All flag combinations work

DOGFOODING:
- Created feat-a01d02e8 to track this work
- All 8 steps completed and marked done
- Using HtmlGraph to improve HtmlGraph!

Feature: feat-a01d02e8 (completed)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, etc.) referencing it.
